Sense.Definition: partikkel som brukes til å danne genitiv
used in expressions to express displeasure
sound used to imitate a caw (crow call)
the sound of a bang (especially gunshots)
some times, once in a while
out of, from (only used in some Latin-based expressions)
abracadabra (said when doing a magic trick)
on top of, on the surface of
used to give a hesitant answer; neither yes nor no
used as an amplified version of "jo"
better (used to amplify; as in "you better stop")
used to express contempt or blame
used to express disagreement or contempt
used to imitate a duck or a frog
used to imitate the sound of frogs or ducks
used to repeat a beeping sound
exclamation said to one who sneezes ("Bless you!")
oh (used as an exclamation of joy, fear, surprise, etc.)
used to make a horse stop
oh (used as an exclamation of joy, fear, surprise, etc.)
used when something is overwhelming or surprising
the sound of a gun going off
used to express a weak, beeping sound
used to mimic the sound of a bell
a plump sound, like a rock falling into water
on top of, on the surface of
used to make a horse stop
used to replicate laughter or giggling
around (in different parts of a place)
used to imitate the sound of clicking
to the rear of (on a ship)
used to express surprise or schadenfreude
used to express contempt or disgust
yum (used to indicate something tastes good)
used to mimic the sound of a loud bang
used to mimic the sound of something hitting water
towards the lower part of something
used to imitate a sudden, loud sound
to, up, on, for (especially in expressions borrowed from French)
exclamation said to one who sneezes ("Bless you!")
used to express a sudden understanding of something
à la; in the style/manner of
better (used to amplify; as in "you better stop")
used to replicate the sound of snapping a photo
the other (out of two, when used in singular); the others (out of several, when used in plural)
used to imitate the sound of a small bell
something said to children to make them sleep
used to express a neutral feeling towards something
used to mimic the sound of a splash
sound one makes when in pain
sound used to express unexpected disappointment
used for something that happens suddenly
used to express laughter or joy
used to imitate the sound of something hitting something else
à la; in the style/manner of
used to indicate that something is decided
better (used to amplify; as in "you better stop")
down by; via somewhere that lies further down from one's location
used as an expression of glee or surprise
used to express surprise, anger or other emotions; damn, darn, shit, fuck, hell
just as, in the same moment
to, up, on, for (especially in expressions borrowed from French)
abracadabra (said when doing a magic trick)
used to express sorrow, pain or condemnation
thump (used to imitate sound of something hitting/falling)
used to imitate the sound of a doorbell
down by; via somewhere that lies further down from one's location
in a place that is further down
yes (in opposition to a negatively phrased question)
used to mimic the sound of a (steam) train
used to imitate the sound of a duck or goose
used to replicate the sound of clearing one's throat
